title: Stage 4: workflow-data generation script (mine prompts -> generate -> verify -> jsonl)
description: A runnable script that loads mined git records, backtranslates user prompts, generates plan-first iterative-REPL workflows with execution verification, and writes training JSONL. Exposes --git-data, --target, --min-pass-rate, --no-verify-execution.

title: Stage 2: workflow generation (goal/files/plan + iterative REPL with failures)
description: From a mined user prompt, generate the full agent trace: a plan pass (goal, files, ordered steps) then a workflow pass that builds one function at a time in the REPL, deliberately showing a failure-and-recovery arc, ending in a unified diff. Self-contained/executable. New training system prompt frames the agent's job. Output = the whole workflow; input = user prompt + one-line project context.

title: Stage 1: mine user-style prompts by backtranslating commit messages
description: From the mined git data, filter substantive commit messages and backtranslate each into a vague, goal-level user request plus a one-line project context (PlanSearch-style backtranslation). Produces the seed-prompt pool for workflow generation.

title: Redesign synthetic data to teach the full agent REPL workflow
description: Teach the LoRA the end-to-end workflow: general user prompt -> figure out goal -> lay out files -> sketch plan -> build iteratively in the REPL (with real failures and recovery) -> final diff. Grounded in RPG (plan-first, topological TDD per node) and PlanSearch (NL sketch dominates success). Input = vague user request (+ one-line project context), output = the whole workflow. Seed prompts backtranslated from commit messages. Failures are a feature: keep error->fix steps, verify the session reaches a correct end state.

title: bb_eval runs forms in repo cwd; file-writing forms pollute the project
description: The babashka eval harness runs the subprocess in the repo working directory, so generated forms that (spit ...)/write files create stray files in the project root and src/ (e.g. project.clj, src/core.clj, *.edn, *.txt). Run bb with cwd set to a temp directory so relative file writes are sandboxed and cleaned up. Existing stray artifacts from prior runs need manual removal.

title: Feature-tree evolution is a no-op: key mismatch drops all evolved nodes
description: evolve_breadth reads cat.get('category') but the LLM returns categories keyed 'name', so every evolved node is skipped (cat_id empty -> continue). Tree stays at baseline size (71->71 nodes), which caps synthetic task generation (only 173 tasks at target 300) and removes EpiCoder's key diversity lever. Check evolve_depth/evolve_detail for the same mismatch; make key extraction tolerant of name/category/id/label.

title: Stage B: ground synthetic solutions in real eval results
description: Parse a generated REPL solution (;; eval:/;; result: blocks + ;; apply: diff), extract the eval forms (multi-line aware), run them through the bb_eval harness, replace fabricated ;; result: lines with real output, and compute a pass_rate (fraction of forms that execute cleanly). Foundation for filtering in the pipeline.

title: Stage A: babashka eval harness (run forms, capture real results)
description: A module that evaluates a sequence of Clojure forms in a single babashka session (state persists via load-string), capturing each form's return value, stdout, and ok/error status. Timeout-protected subprocess. Foundation for grounding synthetic REPL traces in real execution.

title: Execution-verified synthetic generation via babashka
description: Both EpiCoder (2.3 iterative refinement) and IQuest (4.1/4.2 sandboxed execution) make execution-based verification the central quality lever. The synthetic pipeline currently fabricates ;; result: lines. Wire real babashka execution: run the generated eval forms, replace results with real output, and filter solutions whose forms error. Requires steering generation toward self-contained, runnable forms (no phantom project namespaces).

title: Trim git-mined before-state to changed regions (not whole files)
description: Triplet mining inlines the full before-state of every file touched across the arc, so 70% of examples exceed the 50k char filter (median 74k, max 3.1MB). For a LoRA at 8-32k context this is untrainable. Trim the rendered input to the changed top-level forms (the functions/hunks being edited) plus the ns form, using the diff hunk line ranges, instead of whole files. Keep the full before-state in MinedExample; trim only at to_dict().

title: Rework miner to IQuest-style code-flow triplets
description: Adopt the IQuest-Coder git-history recipe (Tech Report 3.1): (1) lifecycle windowing — sample commits from the 40-80% percentile of project life (mature/stable phase) instead of the most-recent N from HEAD; (2) multi-iteration (R_old, P, R_new) triplets via forward-search spans rather than single-commit before/after; (3) endpoint quality filtering (non-empty Clojure diff, meaningful combined message). Keeps the existing output schema; format change (drop fabricated REPL blocks) tracked separately.

title: DeepSeekProvider doubles /v1 in endpoint URL -> 404
description: deepseek.py built the endpoint as base_url + '/v1/chat/completions'. The synthetic pipeline's setup_llm passed base_url=https://api.deepseek.com/v1, producing .../v1/v1/chat/completions and a 404, so the synthetic generator could not call DeepSeek at all. Normalize base_url (strip trailing /v1) and fix the script default.

title: Assembler has no max-length filter; 1.2MB inputs reach training set
description: The codeflow assembly pipeline (load -> dedup -> balance -> format -> validate) never filters records by length. A full mine of the 22 repos yields inputs up to 1.2MB (~300K tokens) and 232/511 records over 30KB at --max-per-type 150. These blow past any training context window and get silently truncated, wasting the example. Add a max-length drop (or truncation) step in assemble_dataset, configurable via CLI (e.g. --max-input-chars). The validator already scores records but does not drop them.

title: 5b: Create tool-calling support — read_file, write_file, apply_diff, search_codebase tools
5a: Create code generator — multi-file context management, unified diff parsing from model output
4b: Create LoRA config presets — Qwen3.6-27B, rank 64, targets all-linear, LR 3e-4, cosine schedule, 3 epochs
4c: Create RunPod training script scripts/train_code_lora.py — LLaMA-Factory on A100/H100, 8K context, BF16 LoRA
4a: Create training data loader — load JSONL, pack multi-file examples within 128K context (target: 2K-3K examples)
3b: Create LLaMA-Factory formatter — instruction/response JSONL with multi-file context, system prompt with Mycelium architecture
3a: Create dataset assembler — merge git-mined and synthetic data, deduplicate, balance by change type
2h: Create prompt files — all Clojure-adapted prompts (extract, evolve, gen_question, gen_code, analysis)
2f: Create code generation — gen_code.py adapted for .clj/.cljs files with clojure.test format, two-pass analysis+code prompt
2e: Create question generation — gen_question.py adapted with Clojure scenarios (Ring middleware, reagent components, Datomic migrations, core.async pipelines)
2c: Create feature tree construction + evolution — construct_tree.py and feature_evol.py with Clojure taxonomy, Mycelium architecture patterns
2b: Create Clojure feature extraction — extract_features.py adapted with Clojure-specific prompt categories (macros, protocols, core.async, spec, etc.)
2a: Copy coreset clustering from EpiCoder — kcenter_greedy.py, sampling_def.py, cluster/main.py
1d: Create diff parser — parse unified diffs into structured {file, old, new, hunks}
1c: Create Python git miner driver — clone repos, invoke git log, call Clojure subprocess, collect output
1b: Create Clojure-specific filter predicates — detect pure-function refactoring, state-machine patterns, side-effect isolation
1a: Create Clojure git processor — tech.ml.dataset filtering pipeline for commit/PR mining with deps.edn
Phase 2: Synthetic Code Data — EpiCoder adaptation for Clojure feature trees, question generation, code generation
Phase 1: Git History Mining — JVM-powered Clojure repo mining (30-50 repos, target 2K-3K qualifying examples)
Scaled workflow-data generation run (apps+libs)
description: Run generate_workflow_data.py over the full git pool (web_apps + full_v3 + new_repos) to produce plan-first iterative-REPL workflow traces at volume. Target ~200 kept after execution-verification. Replace old flat synthetic (scaled_v2) with new workflow-format data in the combined assembly.

title: Add open-source Clojure web app repos (app structure, not just libraries)
description: Current repo set is mostly libraries. Add substantial open-source Clojure web applications (penpot, logseq, athens, netrunner, clojars-web, yetibot, kit) to teach real app structure — integrant components, reitit routes, ring handlers, db access. Enriches both the git transition stream and the backtranslated prompt pool.

title: Combined assembly: tag record source (git vs synthetic) for composition reporting
description: To see the combined dataset end-to-end, assemble_dataset should tag each record with its origin (git/synthetic) so we can report the mix after dedup/balance/filter. The tag is transient — the format step strips it for the clean training JSONL — but lets the pipeline report git/synth split and per-source quality.

title: Git-mined output: drop fabricated REPL eval blocks, emit transition format
description: Per the IQuest/EpiCoder eval-block insight: git-mined examples currently fabricate ;; eval:/;; result: REPL traces (the <args> placeholders) on top of real diffs. Split objectives — git-mined teaches the (before-state, instruction, diff) transition (Aider/SWE-bench shape); real REPL trajectories come from the execution-verified synthetic path. Remove the fabricated REPL blocks from MinedExample output; verify formatter/validator stay green.

title: Miner crashes on non-UTF-8 git blobs (babashka yields 0 examples)
description: mine_clojure_repos.py over babashka fails with "'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0x9e in position 9710: invalid start byte" and produces 0 examples for that repo (1 failed mine out of 22). The git blob/diff reading decodes as strict UTF-8; some repos have non-UTF-8 bytes (binary or latin-1 content). Decode with errors='replace' or skip binary blobs so one bad file doesn't drop the whole repo.

title: 5d: Inference tests — verify adapter loading, code generation quality, tool parsing
5c: Create interactive CLI scripts/code_agent.py — multi-file editing agent
4e: End-to-end training test — small dataset, verify loss convergence, adapter loading
4d: Update MLX conversion + model fusion scripts for Qwen3.6-27B LoRA adapters (local inference after cloud training)
3d: CLI entry point scripts/assemble_codeflow_dataset.py + tests
3c: Create dataset validator — valid Clojure syntax check, coherent diffs, instruction relevance scoring
2i: CLI entry point + tests for synthetic data pipeline
2g: Create save_clj.py — adapted from EpiCoder save_py.py for Clojure file verification on disk
2d: Copy tree utilities — tree_utils.py (sampling/merge) and file_utils.py (JSONL sort/merge) from EpiCoder
1g: Tests for git miner, diff parser, session grouper — pytest with minimal fixtures
1f: CLI entry point scripts/mine_clojure_repos.py — end-to-end mining workflow
1e: Create session grouper — group commits by PR boundary, time proximity, commit message prefix
Phase 3: Assembly & Formatting — Merge git-mined and synthetic data, format for LLaMA-Factory
Phase 4: LoRA Training — Fine-tune Qwen3.6-27B with LoRA on RunPod (A100/H100)
6c: Run full test suite after archival — ensure nothing broken
6b: Verify shared infrastructure intact — llm/, utils/, generation framework, config.py, MLX/merge scripts
6a: Move style-transfer code to archive/ — persona/, rag/, vocabulary/, validation/, repl/, old prompts, old scripts
Phase 6: Archive Style-Transfer Code — Move legacy code to archive/, keep shared infrastructure
Phase 5: Inference Pipeline — Code editing agent with multi-file tool parsing
